title: Queen Glued to Her King
setup: The black queen on **d2** has strayed in front of the white rook, with her king behind her on d8.
hint: When an enemy piece blocks a line to its own king, that piece is pinned — pile in.
explanation:
## The Idea

Despite the hint elsewhere mentioning "skewer," this is technically an **absolute pin**: the black queen on d2 stands between the white rook on d1 and the black king on d8. She cannot move without exposing her king.

White plays {{Rxd2+}}, taking the queen with check.

- {{Kxd2}}? The king is on d8, six squares away — impossible.
- Black must move the king: {{Kc7}}, {{Kc8}}, {{Ke7}}, {{Ke8}}, or {{Kd7}}. Any flight square works since the rook is unprotected and far away.
- The queen is gone for nothing in return.

## Pattern to Memorize

> Pin geometry: **attacker — target — defender (king)** on the same line.
> Skewer geometry: **attacker — king — target** on the same line.

Here the king is *behind* the queen, so it is a pin. Either way, the result is the same: free material.
